summary: DHH highlights Dmitry Sabanin's PrettyException project, which makes web application internals more transparent during development by providing detailed error information. He argues that transparent error reporting is a critical component of any web framework. The post emphasizes the importance of the developer feedback loop: spotting errors quickly, getting sufficient diagnostic information, and minimizing the fix-test cycle time.
key_points: Transparent error reporting transforms web apps from black boxes into debuggable systems
The quality of a web framework depends heavily on its developer feedback loop: error visibility, diagnostic detail, and fix-test cycle speed
Development-mode tooling like PrettyException should be toggled off for production
money_quotes: It's an attempt to turn web-app box from black to white by making the internals more transparent.

This is indeed a critical component of any web-application framework. How easy is it to spot an error, how much information are you supplied to locate it, and how much time does the fix-test cycle take.
key_insight: A web framework's quality is defined by how transparently it surfaces errors and how fast it enables the fix-test cycle during development.
